regime_justification: USDJPY at 161.54 (1986 lows) despite BOJ hiking to 1% last week; MOF verbal interventions failing, spread at 181 bps still wide, but vol compressed at ~7% and carry trade intact—pre-intervention watch-line reached

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY at MOF watch line (161.5), Finance Minister Katayama issuing repeated verbal warnings, yen erasing all April intervention gains, vol bid but not spiking—classic pre-intervention setup

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: MOF at pain threshold, BOJ hiked but yen ignored it; intervention risk elevated but not imminent absent sharp acceleration

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Carry trade grinding higher despite 25bp hike; Fed hawkish tilt widens policy gap; no catalyst inside window but fragility building

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: Next BOJ meeting not until July; absent exogenous shock or USDJPY >162, regime remains stretched but stable

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: If no intervention by then, market will have faded MOF credibility; alternatively, successful intervention resets to stage 0-1

bottom_line: Stage 2 (Pre-Intervention): USDJPY at 161.5 near 1986 lows, MOF issuing daily warnings, yen surrendering all April intervention gains despite BOJ hiking to 1% last week. Carry trade unfazed by 25bp move—181bp spread still wide, vol subdued at 7%, risk assets bid. Classic watch-line setup but trigger absent: pace moderate (+0.67% weekly), vol not spiking, no BOJ meeting until July. Grade C across 1-3w reflects elevated intervention risk and fragile positioning, but no imminent catalyst. Applied unwind prior as tiebreaker: data ambiguous (hike ignored vs. no panic), lean one notch more alert given MOF rhetoric and 1986-low psychology.

Hiked 25bp to 1% Jun 16 but market faded it; next meeting July, no interim signal—surprise unlikely near-term but normalization path confirmed

trigger_map.trigger: USDJPY 1M risk reversals flipping to JPY-call premium
trigger_map.note: Vol subdued at 7% vs. April 16%; no skew flip yet—market not pricing intervention despite watch-line breach

trigger_map.note: At 2.64%, well below prior highs; BOJ signaled continued hikes but pace gradual—no breakout imminent
At 65, rates vol compressed; no regime change in bond market—carry mechanics still favorable

trigger_map.trigger: HY credit spread widening >50bps in a week
trigger_map.note: OAS ~300bp, near cycle tights; no contagion signal, risk appetite strong—credit channel dormant

narrative.p1: USDJPY touched 161.93 intraday, the highest since 1986, before settling at 161.54—erasing all gains from the April 30 intervention despite the BOJ hiking 25bp to 1% last week. Finance Minister Katayama issued repeated verbal warnings about 'excessive moves' and readiness to act, but the market called the bluff: yen weakened +0.67% over 5 days, a moderate grind rather than a spike. Overnight saw US-Iran Hormuz de-escalation narrative support risk-on (VIX +2.3% but still 16.78), oil consolidating at $77, and Treasuries flat (10Y 4.46%). BOJ Deputy Governor Himino reiterated Friday that real rates remain 'extremely low' and further hikes are coming, but the market is pricing only ~67% odds of another move by December. Stage remains 2 (Pre-Intervention): watch-line breached, verbal intervention active, but no kinetic action yet and pace not spiking. No grade change from yesterday's C.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors show a fragile but not breaking setup. US-JP spread at 182bp is 32bp wider than Jan low (150bp)—wide enough to sustain carry but narrowing post-hike. Carry-to-vol proxy (spread/vol) at ~26 is elevated, signaling complacency: vol at 7% is well below April's 16% despite being at the same USDJPY level MOF intervened at in April. Distance from 60d mean: USDJPY now ~2% above 158.76 SMA—stretched but not extreme. USDJPY pace at +0.67% weekly is below MOF's historical intervention threshold (~1%+ daily moves). The tell: BOJ hiked, JGBs sold off 1bp, yen ignored it entirely. That's a policy-impotence signal, not a carry-unwind signal. We're in Domino Stage 2 because the setup is primed—MOF at watch line, verbal warnings daily, intervention gains erased—but the trigger is missing: no pace spike, no vol explosion, no risk-off cascade. 1-2w grades stay C (Watchful) because intervention risk is elevated and one headline can flip this, but data is not screaming imminent. **Applied unwind prior**: data is genuinely ambiguous (hike ignored = unwind setup, but vol/credit/equities = risk-on intact). Lean C rather than D as tiebreaker given MOF rhetoric intensity and 1986-low psychology creating fragile positioning. Without the prior, would grade D (Unlikely) for 1w.
narrative.p3: BTC at $64,245, up +0.3% on the session, consolidating the $61k-$65k range established post-Fed. Open interest direction unavailable but price action suggests neither breakout nor breakdown imminent—liquidity sympathy to risk-on is neutral. BTC-DXY correlation elevated (DXY holding 100.85 despite oil pullback), meaning BTC is trading as a dollar-positive asset, not a safe-haven. For crypto positioning: if yen carry unwinds (Stage 3+), expect BTC to drain alongside equities as levered long positions unwind and USD liquidity tightens. Current read: no stress, but no breakout fuel either. If USDJPY intervention occurs and succeeds (yen rallies 3-5%), expect short-term BTC weakness as dollar-longs get hit. If intervention fails (yen fades gains within 48h like in April/May), BTC likely consolidates or grinds higher with continued risk-on. Key watch: $60k support—break below signals liquidity stress; hold above keeps neutral bias.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: 1w vs 2w both C: near-term intervention risk elevated (MOF at watch line, daily warnings, 1986-low psychology) but no catalyst inside 7 days absent sharp USDJPY acceleration or exogenous shock. 2w extends same risk as no BOJ meeting until July and Fed hiked expectations (9/19 officials) widen policy gap further, keeping carry intact unless MOF acts. Difference: 2w allows more time for pace to accelerate or geopolitical shock (Hormuz reversal, US-Iran talks collapse) to trigger Stage 3.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: 2w C vs 3w C: both Watchful but 3w scenario-weights intervention probability lower as time passes without action. If MOF hasn't acted by 2w mark despite USDJPY >161, market will interpret as reluctance (April/May pattern: intervene, fade, repeat verbal only). 3w sits inside 'waiting for next BOJ meeting' zone (July)—unless USDJPY >162 or pace >1%/day forces MOF hand, status quo grinds higher. Risk: 3w also captures any July BOJ pre-meeting leak or Ueda speech that could shift expectations.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: 3w C vs 4w D: by 4w (late July), either intervention occurred and reset to Stage 0-1, or MOF credibility is spent and market has faded verbal warnings entirely. Grade D (Unlikely) reflects scenario where no intervention = market calls bluff, carry grinds higher, Stage 2 reverts to Stage 1 (Pressure Building but not Pre-Intervention). Alternatively, if intervention succeeds by week 3, 4w grade would be F (unwind averted). Non-monotonic drop reflects binary outcomes resolving by late July: intervention success/failure or BOJ July meeting shifts trajectory.

watch_list: USDJPY daily pace: >1% move in a session historically triggers MOF action—watch for headline-driven spike (Hormuz, Fed official hawkish comment, BOJ dissent leak)
MOF rate-check rumors: Japanese press (Nikkei, Jiji) reporting MOF checking FX levels with banks is 24-48h leading indicator of intervention
USDJPY 1M risk reversal skew: currently flat at ~7% vol; flip to JPY-call premium would signal options market pricing intervention
BOJ Himino/Ueda speeches: any signal of accelerated normalization or explicit FX concern would shift market pricing of July meeting
WTI crude: bounce back above $80 would re-escalate import-cost pressure, giving BOJ/MOF dual mandate (inflation + FX) to act
